examples.title: Basic printf() Examples
examples.description: Demonstrates fundamental printf() usage with various format specifiers
examples.explanation: Shows basic printf() usage: %d for integers, %f for floats, %c for characters, %s for strings. Demonstrates newline escape sequence and multiple format specifiers in one printf call.

examples.title: Field Width and Precision
examples.description: Illustrates field width and precision specifiers in printf()
examples.explanation: Demonstrates field width (%Nd) for minimum N characters with padding, precision (%.Nf) for decimal places, and %.Ns for limiting string characters. Shows right-alignment, left-alignment (-), and zero-padding (0).

examples.title: Escape Sequences in printf()
examples.description: Shows various escape sequences for formatting output
examples.explanation: Escape sequences: \n (newline), \t (tab), \\ (backslash), \" (double quote), %% (literal percent), \r (carriage return). These allow printing special characters that cannot be typed directly.

examples.title: Return Value of printf()
examples.description: Demonstrates how to use printf() return value
examples.explanation: printf() returns the number of characters successfully written to output (excluding null terminator). This can be used for debugging, error checking, or determining output length. Returns negative value on error.
